name: Mocha Chocolate Torte
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease two 9-inch cake pans.
2. For the cake: In a small bowl, mix cocoa powder and boiling water until smooth. Set aside to cool.
3. In a large mixing bowl, cream together sugar and butter until light and fluffy. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in vanilla extract.
4. Sift together flour, baking soda, and salt; add alternately with the buttermilk into the butter mixture, stirring until just combined.
5. Pour batter into prepared pans and bake for 35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Allow to cool completely in pans on wire racks.
6. For the filling: In a saucepan, cook flour and milk over low heat, stirring constantly, until thickened. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
7. In a large bowl, cream sugar and butter until light. Beat in vanilla extract.
8. Add cooled flour mixture to the butter mixture, alternating with the coffee dissolved in water, beating well after each addition.
9. Fold in cocoa powder and pecans.
10. Split cakes horizontally into thirds. Place one layer on a serving plate; spread with filling. Top with remaining layers and fillings.
11. For the frosting: In a large bowl, beat shortening and butter until creamy. Gradually add evaporated milk, water, vanilla extract, and salt, beating well.
12. Add sifted confectioners' sugar gradually until desired consistency is reached. Spread over top and sides of cake.
13. Decorate with pecan halves if desired. Serve at room temperature.
Yield: About 16 servings.
